idea-plugin.name: Pre-Push Compilation Checker
idea-plugin.description:
Prevents pushing code with compilation errors to your repository.

How it works:
  • IDE push (Git > Push dialog): Uses IntelliJ's real prePushHandler extension point. It first checks whether the IDE already knows about problems in the files being pushed, then compiles only the changed source files. A full project build is used only when build files or deletions make a narrow compile unsafe.
  • Terminal / external git push: On project open, the plugin automatically installs a pre-push Git hook that skips non-code pushes, honors an optional PRE_PUSH_CHECKER_COMMAND, and otherwise runs a fast Gradle/Maven compile command before allowing the push.
Works with Gradle (wrapper or system), Maven (wrapper or system), and mixed Java/Kotlin JVM projects.
